metadata.notes: Pull request history for release and maintenance work.

prs.title: fix(loan): Guarantor validation crashes when guarantor list is empty
prs.body:
Refs #1.

We are seeing a crash in micro_admin/models.py when a loan is saved without any guarantors. The record imports fine, but the edit form explodes on submit when the guarantor section is left blank.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Open an existing loan
2. Clear the guarantor list
3. Save the form

**Expected**
The form should validate and ask for a guarantor if the rule requires one.

**Observed**
The page returns a 500 and the form does not recover gracefully.

**Notes**
This started showing up after the latest repayment rule changes.

review.reviewer_comment: Can you add a regression test for the empty path?
review.author_reply: Good catch. I have added the missing guard and a focused test.

prs.title: fix(payment): Payment form accepts zero amount on retry
prs.body:
Refs #2.

A support user reported that micro_admin/forms.py allows a zero-value payment when the submit button is clicked twice in a row. The first submit succeeds, the second one should be rejected but currently slips through.

**Expected**
A duplicate submission should be blocked.

**Observed**
The payment is recorded twice, once with amount and once with zero.

**Context**
This is more visible on slower connections and after the form gets resubmitted from the browser back button.

review.reviewer_comment: This looks good structurally, but I would split the validation from the save logic.
review.author_reply: Yep, that was the missing branch. Fixed in the latest commit.

prs.title: fix(ledger): Ledger totals drift after deleting a stale receipt
prs.body:
Refs #3.

Users noticed that totals shown by micro_admin/views.py no longer match the receipt summary after a stale receipt is removed. The mismatch only appears in branches with several backdated entries.

**Expected**
Summary and detail rows should agree.

**Observed**
The total drops by one entry even though the item was only marked inactive.

**Additional detail**
The issue is reproducible in the month-end report for branch NAGPUR-02.

review.reviewer_comment: Small nit: please name the helper a little more clearly.
review.author_reply: Thanks, I simplified the path and added coverage for the null case.

prs.title: fix(receipt): Receipt list duplicates row after retry flow
prs.body:
Refs #4.

While testing the retry flow in micro_admin/forms.py, the same receipt appears twice after a failed print attempt. The duplicate is not visible in the database, only in the rendered page.

**Expected**
One row per receipt.

**Observed**
The render shows the receipt twice if the page is refreshed immediately after submit.

review.reviewer_comment: I verified the main branch, but can you confirm what happens when the queryset is empty?
review.author_reply: Done. I also tightened the error message so support can spot it faster.

prs.title: fix(client): Client update loses branch selection after save
prs.body:
Refs #5.

Updating client details in micro_admin/forms.py resets the branch dropdown if the user edits an address field first. The change is subtle but a few branch admins spotted it during daily use.

**Steps**
Open a client, change address, switch branch, save, then reopen.

**Expected**
The selected branch should stay attached to the record.

**Observed**
The branch falls back to the default one.

review.reviewer_comment: Thanks for addressing the edge case. Approving after the follow-up commit.
review.author_reply: Addressed in the follow-up commit and pushed the docs update.

prs.title: fix(savings): Savings withdrawal can go negative on partial edit
prs.body:
Refs #6.

An edit path in savings/views.py does not re-check the account balance before saving a withdrawal. This is only visible when an existing transaction is reopened and changed from the list view.

**Expected**
The balance should never fall below zero.

**Observed**
A negative amount is accepted and the ledger is off by one transaction.

review.reviewer_comment: One more pass on the error message would help support a lot.
review.author_reply: Good catch. I have added the missing guard and a focused test.

prs.title: fix(group): Group interest calc uses old rate after approval
prs.body:
Refs #7.

When a group loan moves from pending to approved, micro_admin/models.py keeps the previous rate until the page is reloaded. That makes the first preview misleading and the branch manager has to refresh manually.

**Expected**
The new rate should be reflected immediately.

**Observed**
The old percentage remains in the preview pane.

review.reviewer_comment: Could you keep the formatting consistent with the adjacent form handlers?
review.author_reply: Yep, that was the missing branch. Fixed in the latest commit.

prs.title: feat(loan): Add installment preview to loan workflow
prs.body:
Refs #8.

It would help the field team if micro_admin/models.py showed a lightweight installment preview before the final save. This is mostly about avoiding a second call back to the office when the calculation looks off.

**Desired behavior**
Show a preview block with amount, tenure, and the next due date.

**Why now**
Several branches are asking for this while training new staff.

review.reviewer_comment: Can you add a regression test for the empty path?
review.author_reply: Thanks, I simplified the path and added coverage for the null case.

prs.title: feat(ledger): Export ledger with optional branch filter
prs.body:
Refs #9.

Users running monthly reports asked for a branch filter in micro_admin/views.py. Right now they have to export the full ledger and filter it manually in a spreadsheet.

**Acceptance criteria**
- branch filter is optional
- existing export stays unchanged when the filter is empty
- the output still matches the current PDF layout

review.reviewer_comment: This looks good structurally, but I would split the validation from the save logic.
review.author_reply: Done. I also tightened the error message so support can spot it faster.

prs.title: feat(payment): Improve payment form hints for first-time operators
prs.body:
Refs #10.

Operators asked for a clearer hint set in micro_admin/forms.py, especially around date entry and amount rounding. The form is usable today, but the warnings are easy to miss.

**Desired outcome**
Add inline guidance without changing the underlying validation rules.

review.reviewer_comment: Small nit: please name the helper a little more clearly.
review.author_reply: Addressed in the follow-up commit and pushed the docs update.

prs.title: feat(receipt): Allow receipt reprint from the list page
prs.body:
Refs #11.

Support asked for a quick reprint action in micro_admin/forms.py. The current flow forces users to open the receipt first, which is fine on desktop but annoying on smaller screens.

**Requested behavior**
Add a reprint action to the row menu.

review.reviewer_comment: I verified the main branch, but can you confirm what happens when the queryset is empty?
review.author_reply: Good catch. I have added the missing guard and a focused test.

prs.title: feat(client): Make client search remember the last branch
prs.body:
Refs #12.

Branch users want micro_admin/forms.py to remember the last branch they were working in. It would save them from reselecting the same branch dozens of times a day.

**Request**
Persist the branch choice per user session.

review.reviewer_comment: Thanks for addressing the edge case. Approving after the follow-up commit.
review.author_reply: Yep, that was the missing branch. Fixed in the latest commit.

prs.title: docs(docs): Should we document the branch approval flow?
prs.body:
Discussion for #13.

We keep answering the same support question about who can approve a loan and where the approval history lives. Should we add a short section to README.rst before the next rollout?

**Context**
The answer seems obvious to the team, but not to the branch office.

review.reviewer_comment: One more pass on the error message would help support a lot.
review.author_reply: Thanks, I simplified the path and added coverage for the null case.

prs.title: docs(docs): Docs: add setup notes for local branch testing
prs.body:
Refs #14.

The onboarding notes in README.rst are missing the steps for local branch testing. This keeps coming up when someone clones the app and tries to run it against a test database.

**Scope**
Add the missing steps and a short troubleshooting section.

review.reviewer_comment: Could you keep the formatting consistent with the adjacent form handlers?
review.author_reply: Done. I also tightened the error message so support can spot it faster.

review.author_reply: Addressed in the follow-up commit and pushed the docs update.

prs.title: feat(ledger): Export ledger with optional branch filter
prs.body:
Refs #51.

Users running monthly reports asked for a branch filter in micro_admin/views.py. Right now they have to export the full ledger and filter it manually in a spreadsheet.

**Acceptance criteria**
- branch filter is optional
- existing export stays unchanged when the filter is empty
- the output still matches the current PDF layout

review.reviewer_comment: This looks good structurally, but I would split the validation from the save logic.
review.author_reply: Good catch. I have added the missing guard and a focused test.

prs.title: feat(payment): Improve payment form hints for first-time operators
prs.body:
Refs #52.

Operators asked for a clearer hint set in micro_admin/forms.py, especially around date entry and amount rounding. The form is usable today, but the warnings are easy to miss.

**Desired outcome**
Add inline guidance without changing the underlying validation rules.

review.reviewer_comment: Small nit: please name the helper a little more clearly.
review.author_reply: Yep, that was the missing branch. Fixed in the latest commit.

prs.title: feat(receipt): Allow receipt reprint from the list page
prs.body:
Refs #53.

Support asked for a quick reprint action in micro_admin/forms.py. The current flow forces users to open the receipt first, which is fine on desktop but annoying on smaller screens.

**Requested behavior**
Add a reprint action to the row menu.

review.reviewer_comment: I verified the main branch, but can you confirm what happens when the queryset is empty?
review.author_reply: Thanks, I simplified the path and added coverage for the null case.

prs.title: feat(client): Make client search remember the last branch
prs.body:
Refs #54.

Branch users want micro_admin/forms.py to remember the last branch they were working in. It would save them from reselecting the same branch dozens of times a day.

**Request**
Persist the branch choice per user session.

review.reviewer_comment: Thanks for addressing the edge case. Approving after the follow-up commit.
review.author_reply: Done. I also tightened the error message so support can spot it faster.

prs.title: docs(docs): Should we document the branch approval flow?
prs.body:
Discussion for #55.

We keep answering the same support question about who can approve a loan and where the approval history lives. Should we add a short section to README.rst before the next rollout?

**Context**
The answer seems obvious to the team, but not to the branch office.

review.reviewer_comment: One more pass on the error message would help support a lot.
review.author_reply: Addressed in the follow-up commit and pushed the docs update.

prs.title: docs(docs): Docs: add setup notes for local branch testing
prs.body:
Refs #56.

The onboarding notes in README.rst are missing the steps for local branch testing. This keeps coming up when someone clones the app and tries to run it against a test database.

**Scope**
Add the missing steps and a short troubleshooting section.

review.reviewer_comment: Could you keep the formatting consistent with the adjacent form handlers?
review.author_reply: Good catch. I have added the missing guard and a focused test.

prs.title: fix(loan): Guarantor validation crashes when guarantor list is empty
prs.body:
Refs #57.

We are seeing a crash in micro_admin/models.py when a loan is saved without any guarantors. The record imports fine, but the edit form explodes on submit when the guarantor section is left blank.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Open an existing loan
2. Clear the guarantor list
3. Save the form

**Expected**
The form should validate and ask for a guarantor if the rule requires one.

**Observed**
The page returns a 500 and the form does not recover gracefully.

**Notes**
This started showing up after the latest repayment rule changes.

review.reviewer_comment: Can you add a regression test for the empty path?
review.author_reply: Yep, that was the missing branch. Fixed in the latest commit.

prs.title: fix(payment): Payment form accepts zero amount on retry
prs.body:
Refs #58.

A support user reported that micro_admin/forms.py allows a zero-value payment when the submit button is clicked twice in a row. The first submit succeeds, the second one should be rejected but currently slips through.

**Expected**
A duplicate submission should be blocked.

**Observed**
The payment is recorded twice, once with amount and once with zero.

**Context**
This is more visible on slower connections and after the form gets resubmitted from the browser back button.

review.reviewer_comment: This looks good structurally, but I would split the validation from the save logic.
review.author_reply: Thanks, I simplified the path and added coverage for the null case.

prs.title: fix(ledger): Ledger totals drift after deleting a stale receipt
prs.body:
Refs #59.

Users noticed that totals shown by micro_admin/views.py no longer match the receipt summary after a stale receipt is removed. The mismatch only appears in branches with several backdated entries.

**Expected**
Summary and detail rows should agree.

**Observed**
The total drops by one entry even though the item was only marked inactive.

**Additional detail**
The issue is reproducible in the month-end report for branch NAGPUR-02.

review.reviewer_comment: Small nit: please name the helper a little more clearly.
review.author_reply: Done. I also tightened the error message so support can spot it faster.

prs.title: fix(receipt): Receipt list duplicates row after retry flow
prs.body:
Refs #60.

While testing the retry flow in micro_admin/forms.py, the same receipt appears twice after a failed print attempt. The duplicate is not visible in the database, only in the rendered page.

**Expected**
One row per receipt.

**Observed**
The render shows the receipt twice if the page is refreshed immediately after submit.

review.reviewer_comment: I verified the main branch, but can you confirm what happens when the queryset is empty?
review.author_reply: Addressed in the follow-up commit and pushed the docs update.

prs.title: fix(client): Client update loses branch selection after save
prs.body:
Refs #61.

Updating client details in micro_admin/forms.py resets the branch dropdown if the user edits an address field first. The change is subtle but a few branch admins spotted it during daily use.

**Steps**
Open a client, change address, switch branch, save, then reopen.

**Expected**
The selected branch should stay attached to the record.

**Observed**
The branch falls back to the default one.

review.reviewer_comment: Thanks for addressing the edge case. Approving after the follow-up commit.
review.author_reply: Good catch. I have added the missing guard and a focused test.

prs.title: fix(savings): Savings withdrawal can go negative on partial edit
prs.body:
Refs #62.

An edit path in savings/views.py does not re-check the account balance before saving a withdrawal. This is only visible when an existing transaction is reopened and changed from the list view.

**Expected**
The balance should never fall below zero.

**Observed**
A negative amount is accepted and the ledger is off by one transaction.

review.reviewer_comment: One more pass on the error message would help support a lot.
review.author_reply: Yep, that was the missing branch. Fixed in the latest commit.

prs.title: fix(group): Group interest calc uses old rate after approval
prs.body:
Refs #63.

When a group loan moves from pending to approved, micro_admin/models.py keeps the previous rate until the page is reloaded. That makes the first preview misleading and the branch manager has to refresh manually.

**Expected**
The new rate should be reflected immediately.

**Observed**
The old percentage remains in the preview pane.

review.reviewer_comment: Could you keep the formatting consistent with the adjacent form handlers?
review.author_reply: Thanks, I simplified the path and added coverage for the null case.

prs.title: feat(loan): Add installment preview to loan workflow
prs.body:
Refs #64.

It would help the field team if micro_admin/models.py showed a lightweight installment preview before the final save. This is mostly about avoiding a second call back to the office when the calculation looks off.

**Desired behavior**
Show a preview block with amount, tenure, and the next due date.

**Why now**
Several branches are asking for this while training new staff.

review.reviewer_comment: Can you add a regression test for the empty path?
review.author_reply: Done. I also tightened the error message so support can spot it faster.
